raw_consumer_statement:
I opened a Barclays-issued XXXX credit card at the end of XXXX or early XXXX while on a XXXX cruise. Before I received, activated, or used the card, a charge of approximately {$5000.00} appeared on the account dated XX/XX/XXXX. I had made no purchases on that card, onboard or otherwise. My onboard cruise account was linked to another persons payment method, not mine. 

I disputed the {$5000.00} charge with Barclays in early XXXX. Barclays credited the amount back after completing an investigation. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I saw a charge posted on XX/XX/XXXX, a second charge for the same amount ( {$5000.00} ) appeared on my account. It had the same merchant descriptor and the same original transaction date of XX/XX/XXXX, even though it was posted months later. I did not authorize this charge either. 

I contacted Barclays fraud department on XX/XX/XXXX. The representative insisted that I needed to contact XXXX, even though this was an unauthorized charge and Barclays had already credited the first identical charge. The representative could not explain why the same charge was re-posted, would not reopen the fraud investigation, and stated that their prior investigation was complete without providing any written explanation or documentation as required under the Fair Credit Billing Act. 

I was told that Barclays would send documentation within 10 days, but I still have not received anything explaining the basis of their decision or why the charge was posted again. 

I have approximately {$3500.00} in legitimate purchases on the account that I made after receiving the physical card. The new {$5000.00} charge has pushed my balance to roughly {$8500.00}, which is inaccurate and unmanageable. I am now being asked to pay interest on a charge I did not authorize and previously disputed.
